name: Between nature and culture — loop from Hagetmau
name_original: Entre nature et culture — boucle au départ de Hagetmau
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hagetmau traverse the varied landscapes of the Chalosse region in Landes, France. This area is characterized by its rolling hills, offering cyclists more dynamic terrain than the flatter parts of the department. The region features serene lakes, such as Lac d'Agès, and is crossed by the River Louts, providing shaded paths. Extensive forests and open fields contribute to a diverse cycling environment. Best touring cycling routes around Hagetmau The most popular touring cycling route is Between nature and culture — loop from Hagetmau, a 52.6 miles (84.7 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 20 minutes to complete, offering a challenging ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Samadet, a difficult 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path that winds through the hilly terrain and rural scenery of the Chalosse. Local touring cyclists also love the The Chalosse Viewpoint – Saint Martin Church of Audignon loop from Hagetmau, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and offering scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Hagetmau is defined by its hilly Chalosse region, tranquil lakes, and a mix of forests and open fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes. The routes in Hagetmau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hagetmau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Hagetmau?
A: Hagetmau is situated in the Chalosse region, one of the few hilly areas in the Landes department. This means you can expect varied terrain with rolling hills, offering a more dynamic cycling experience compared to flatter regions. Routes often traverse lush greenery, dense forests, and open fields, providing diverse scenery.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available from Hagetmau?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes around Hagetmau are designed as loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284590500/between-nature-and-culture-loop-from-hagetmau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between nature and culture — loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial ride through the local landscape. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24831650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint – Saint Martin Church of Audignon loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic viewpoints.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Hagetmau?
A: The routes around Hagetmau cater to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult. There are 3 easy routes, 34 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes available, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes near Hagetmau?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore cultural and natural landmarks. You might pass by historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4688038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Martin Church of Audignon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3553851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever Abbey&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features serene lakes, such as Lac d'Agès, and the River Louts, providing picturesque views and shaded paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the Hagetmau area?
A: While many routes in the hilly Chalosse region are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy touring cycling routes available around Hagetmau. The area's lakes, like Lacs d'Halco, are also noted for family walks with picnic areas, suggesting gentler paths that might be suitable for family cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Hagetmau?
A: Hagetmau is known for its lush greenery and flowery facades, which are especially vibrant in the summer. This suggests that spring, summer, and early autumn would be ideal for enjoying the scenic routes. The region's commitment to outdoor activities makes it appealing throughout the warmer months.

Q: Are there any long-distance or pilgrimage routes that pass through Hagetmau?
A: Yes, Hagetmau is notably positioned on the &lt;strong&gt;Voie du Vézelay&lt;/strong&gt;, one of the historic pilgrimage paths to Santiago de Compostela. This makes it a significant stop for long-distance touring cyclists following this renowned route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Hagetmau?
A: The touring cycling routes around Hagetmau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain of the hilly Chalosse region, the tranquil lakes, and the mix of forests and open fields that create a diverse and engaging cycling environment.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other activities in Hagetmau?
A: Absolutely. Hagetmau, known as a 'Green City' and a 'sportiest town,' offers various complementary activities. You can enjoy fishing, kayaking, or birdwatching at Lac de Hagetmau, explore the Parc de la Mairie, or visit local markets. Cultural sites like the Prehistosite of Brassempouy are also nearby, allowing for a rich touring experience.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer historical or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24831837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Martin Church of Audignon – Brassempouy Prehistoric Site loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight cultural landmarks. The region also features charming bastide towns and the Crypt of Saint-Girons, offering historical insights along your ride.

Q: What kind of facilities are available for cyclists in Hagetmau?
A: Hagetmau is a cycling-friendly location, even being a starting point for a Tour de France stage in 2026. Local amenities include hotels that cater to cyclists, often offering secure bike parking. The town also has a weekly market where you can experience local gastronomy.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24831650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint – Saint Martin Church of Audignon loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for its scenic viewpoints, allowing you to take in the beauty of the Chalosse landscape.
tips: The classified capitals of the Saint-Girons Crypt are a jewel of the 12th century.

It was in 1732 that “a Royal earthenware factory” was established in the small town of Samadet, which produced earthenware with rich and colorful decorations for more than 100 years.
The factory will employ a workforce with specialized know-how and its production will be widely distributed, notably through the port of Bayonne. But, in 1838, the Manufacture could not resist the competition and closed its doors. The museum, created in 1968 by the Earthenware Committee and given to the Department in 1998, preserves the memory of this epic; it tells the history of Samadet earthenware and that of tableware.
In Pimbo, the church stands on a hillside in the Gabas valley on the pilgrims’ Jacobean route.

waypoints.name: Musée de la Faïence & des Arts de la table - Samadet
